client.name: Brian Chaggu - International Relations Research
client.label: International relations researcher, policy writer, and academic with East Africa, governance, peacebuilding, and public-policy analysis experience
client.work_authorization: Work authorization status to be confirmed

core_positioning.notes: This profile is for international relations, policy research, think-tank, governance, peace and conflict, academic, donor-facing research, and strategic-insight roles. Best-fit targets include Research Fellow, Policy Analyst, Programme Researcher, International Relations Analyst, Africa Programme roles, and academic/research writing positions. Keep this separate from Brian's operations-support profile so the AI does not dilute the research narrative with service-operations language.

summary_base: International relations researcher and policy analyst with advanced academic and applied research experience across East Africa, governance, conflict, civic participation, and inclusive-development themes.

Strongest evidence includes leading multi-country research, producing policy and strategic insight outputs, mentoring junior researchers, publishing peer-reviewed and policy commentary work, and teaching university-level international relations and peace/conflict content.

Best positioned for research fellow, policy analysis, programme research, governance, peace and conflict, and international-affairs roles where analytical rigor, writing, and cross-cultural insight matter.

experience.company: The MEA Institute of Strategic Studies
experience.summary: Senior research role leading regional analysis on East Africa's political, economic, and social developments while shaping programme priorities and analytical outputs.
highlights: **Led multi-country research on East Africa** covering governance, civic participation, inclusive development, and regional socio-political dynamics.
**Produced strategic briefings, synthesis reports, and policy insight outputs** for senior leadership and external audiences.
**Defined research priorities with senior leadership** and aligned programme objectives with institutional strategy.
**Mentored junior researchers on methodology, interpretation, and analytical writing** to strengthen research quality across the team.

experience.summary: Research consulting role focused on leadership development, governance, conflict, public-health welfare, and gender-equality strategy work.
highlights: **Designed research to identify leadership-capacity strategies** for addressing complex societal challenges.
**Analyzed governance systems and accountability structures** to develop evidence-based recommendations.
**Studied conflict dynamics and gender disparities** to support practical policy and programme frameworks.
**Contributed research on health-welfare and social-improvement approaches** through policy-focused analysis and recommendations.

experience.summary: Editorial and writing role producing regional analysis, long-form articles, investigative commentary, and multimedia storytelling focused on East Africa and the Great Lakes region.
highlights: **Produced original analysis and opinion pieces** on regional political, social, and economic developments for diverse audiences.
**Collaborated with the Editor-in-Chief on editorial direction** and platform standards.
**Led podcast content and interview programming** exploring conflict, governance, justice, and regional integration themes.
**Expanded insight delivery through writing, editing, and multimedia storytelling** to support informed public dialogue.

experience.summary: Administrative and programme-support role spanning event delivery, stakeholder coordination, communication support, fundraising assistance, and project execution within a university-linked center.
highlights: **Supported daily centre operations and stakeholder coordination** through scheduling, correspondence, record management, and meeting support.
**Coordinated events, workshops, and conferences** including logistics, participant support, and on-site execution.
**Contributed to media and communications delivery** through press-release drafting, interview coordination, and social-media support.
**Supported project design, fundraising, and grant-writing efforts** including proposal development and outcome reporting.

experience.summary: University teaching and supervision work covering core international relations, peace/conflict content, and MA-thesis guidance.
highlights: **Designed and delivered undergraduate teaching** in Introduction to International Relations and Introduction to Peace and Conflict Resolution.
**Supervised MA thesis seminar work** covering research design, methodology, academic writing, and thesis development.
**Applied case-based teaching across governance, diplomacy, and conflict-resolution topics** in a higher-education setting.

education.note: Dissertation: Traditional African Approaches to Natural Resource Conflict Resolution

education.institution: Dr. Salim Ahmed Salim - Centre for Foreign Relations

certifications: NAWA Best Foreign Doctoral Student Grant / Mentorship Program (2023/2024)
